name: The Exile Route - Gormaz Castle Loop
name_original: Castillo de Gormaz por la tierra de Marquesado — circular desde Berlanga de Duero
description: This 36.9-mile (59.4 km) moderate road cycling route from Berlanga de Duero leads to the historic Castillo de Gormaz with panoramic views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Berlanga De Duero traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, including the Escalote River. The broader Soria province offers diverse natural scenarios, with views of the Castilian countryside. Cyclists encounter varying elevations and historical sites, such as medieval castles, providing a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. The region's terrain includes both well-paved surfaces and more challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Berlanga De Duero The most popular road cycling route is The Exile Route - Gormaz Castle Loop, a 36.9 miles (59.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route passes by the historic Gormaz Castle, a significant Caliphate-style fortress. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The Exile Trail, an easy 19.1 miles (30.8 km) path. This route offers a less demanding ride through the local countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Gormaz Castle – Hermitage of San Miguel de Gormaz loop from Gormaz, a 43.4 miles (69.9 km) trail leading through historical areas, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Berlanga De Duero is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical landmarks like castles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Berlanga De Duero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Berlanga de Duero?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Berlanga de Duero, offering a variety of experiences from easy rides to more challenging ascents through the Castilian countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Berlanga de Duero?
A: The spring months are particularly vibrant for road cycling in Berlanga de Duero, with picturesque views of the countryside. Summer also offers good conditions, especially for routes that might include shaded sections or early morning rides.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Berlanga de Duero?
A: Yes, there are easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079305527/the-exile-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Exile Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 19.1-mile (30.8 km) path that offers a less demanding ride through the local countryside.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gormaz Castle – Hermitage of San Miguel de Gormaz loop from Gormaz&lt;/a&gt; is a 43.4-mile (69.9 km) trail leading through historical areas, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes, featuring substantial climbs. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle – San Esteban de Gormaz loop from Berlanga de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 117.7 km with over 1300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers long-distance routes for those seeking extended rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle – San Esteban de Gormaz loop from Berlanga de Duero&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, stretching almost 118 km and providing a comprehensive tour of the area's landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while road cycling near Berlanga de Duero?
A: Road cycling routes in the area often pass by impressive historical landmarks. You can encounter the formidable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gormaz Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a significant Caliphate-style fortress, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers commanding views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075233640/the-exile-route-gormaz-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Exile Route - Gormaz Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the Gormaz Castle.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the rolling hills and diverse landscapes of Berlanga de Duero provide numerous scenic viewpoints. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic vistas of the Castilian countryside, especially from elevated points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Carrascosa&lt;/a&gt;, which overlooks the Soria plateau.

Q: What natural features can I explore on a road bike in this region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, with routes traversing river valleys like the Escalote River. You can also find routes near the Duero River, and explore areas with significant biodiversity. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2677048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vildé Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6498473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caracena River Canyon&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural features that add to the scenic appeal of the rides.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Berlanga de Duero?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Berlanga de Duero are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075233640/the-exile-route-gormaz-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Exile Route - Gormaz Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gormaz Castle – Hermitage of San Miguel de Gormaz loop from Gormaz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Berlanga de Duero?
A: Over 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Berlanga de Duero's varied terrain. They often appreciate the blend of natural beauty, with rolling hills and river valleys, and the rich historical context provided by medieval castles and other landmarks along the routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Berlanga de Duero?
A: While many routes involve varying elevations, some easier paths can be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079305527/the-exile-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Exile Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is an easy route that offers a less demanding experience through the local countryside.
tips: At the junction between the Duero and the Escalote, this old medieval-looking bridge is a beautiful place to stop and rest. Birds, trees with good shade.
To get to the castle you have to climb a steep ramp, but needless to say, it is worth every pedal stroke to see this fabulous medieval fortress.
The town of Brías is located in what is called the Paramos Sorianos, a landscape of plateaus and small hills that are a few kilometers from Berlanga de Duero.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular racebike route is approximately 59.4 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 41 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castillo de Gormaz cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain, starting with an initial uphill stretch for the first two kilometers from Berlanga de Duero, followed by a descent. While much of the route is paved, some segments, particularly the last 7 kilometers, might include unpaved paths. Be prepared for a steep ramp to reach the Castillo de Gormaz itself, which is rewarding for the panoramic views it offers.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in scenery and history. You'll start in Berlanga de Duero's main square, pass through picturesque Aguilera with its Romanesque church of San Martín, and ride parallel to the Duero River. The highlight is undoubtedly the Gormaz Castle, a historic Caliphal fortress offering immense panoramic views. You'll also pass the Duero River Bridge and the settlement of Brías.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Soria, Spain, the best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the colder conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Berlanga de Duero?
faqs.answer: The route begins and ends in Berlanga de Duero. You can typically find parking in or around the town's main square, which serves as an excellent starting point for this circular tour.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. While it features mostly paved surfaces, there's an initial uphill climb and a steep ascent to Gormaz Castle. Road cyclists with some experience and a good level of fitness will find it manageable and rewarding, but it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or access the general area around the Castillo de Gormaz. However, always check local regulations if you plan to enter any specific historical sites or attractions along the way.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of El Destierro (57% of the route) and also includes parts of the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT (14%) and the Camino del Cid (Senderista) (12%).

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily designed for racebikes on roads and paths, the general area is open. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, kept on a leash, and that you adhere to local regulations regarding pets, especially in towns or near historical sites. Remember to carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Berlanga de Duero, your starting and ending point, offers cafes and restaurants. As you pass through smaller settlements like Brías and Aguilera, you might find limited options, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the ride.

faqs.question: What makes the Castillo de Gormaz particularly significant?
faqs.answer: The Castillo de Gormaz is a Caliphal fortress dating back to the 10th century. It holds the distinction of being the largest defensive construction of the Late Middle Ages in Europe and was a crucial border stronghold between Al-Andalus and Castile. The legendary El Cid also served as its warden, adding to its rich historical importance.
